title: eriophyllum wallacei var. australe
intro: Eriophyllum wallacei var. australe, commonly known as the Sunshine Bush or Western Yarrow, is a perennial plant native to Western Australia, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and fern-like foliage. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and minor infections. The medicinal properties of Sunshine Bush also provide health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Eriophyllum wallacei var. australe can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can be used externally as a cream or salve to aid in skin healing and wound care.
